constraints: Complete the reliable end-to-end factory loop with saves recovery checkpoints banking loot shop strongboxes completion replay performance and diagnostics
Add at least one new room or encounter one new enemy and one new weapon through repeatable pipelines
Do not require foundational-system rewrites for those additions
Use automated smoke and regression coverage save interruption tests and representative performance budgets
Support isolated parallel human and AI-agent branches
Most Stage 2 content may remain coherent temporary art
Pass the player mech one ordinary enemy the elite or upgraded droid two weapons and one factory machine or environment set through the intended real pipeline
Prove representative shading emissive damage destruction animation memory and performance behavior
Document import pivots scale sorting collision animation memory and performance requirements
Allow another artist or AI-assisted workflow to reproduce equivalent content without manual Unity repair
Primary developer creates and documents one reference example
An isolated AI-agent branch or occasional collaborator adds another representative item without foundational help
Integrate through review and automated checks without modifying unrelated core systems
Correct documentation gaps exposed during reproduction
Evaluate merge boundaries and repeatability rather than rescuing a special contribution
Automate required fields ranges identifiers references imports loot progression save compatibility and basic runtime behavior
Cover representative spawn fire damage death completion and performance warnings
Treat automation as safe to review rather than approved
Use playable review for feel readability feedback encounter purpose interaction collision navigation and accessibility
Apply the same gates to human and AI-agent contributions
Build ordinary content from typed ScriptableObject definitions and reusable behavior modules
Keep the reusable module set compact and representative
Allow genuinely novel mechanics through isolated reusable code extensions
Require tests playable validation documentation and save loot diagnostic compatibility
Do not modify unrelated foundational systems for ordinary content
Use typed ScriptableObject assets as canonical runtime and ordinary authoring data
Assign stable human-readable identifiers and generated searchable registries
Generate deterministic text summaries for review
Use CSV or JSON selectively for bulk balance localization loot or repetitive data
Never hand-edit generated files and test deterministic round trips
Keep each content contribution mainly inside an owned feature folder
Generate shared registries lookup tables and build indexes deterministically
Give central balance tables and foundational systems explicit owners
Keep branches small reviewable removable rollback-safe and merge-resistant
Reject duplicate identifiers missing references and incompatible package content
Represent meaningful rooms or encounters as independently testable prefab or additive-scene packages
Define contracts for identity connections encounter lifecycle ownership persistence presentation navigation services rewards and budgets
Use a lightweight authored graph for room connections and mission structure
Allow lightweight corridor and decorative prefabs
Use stable IDs and events instead of fragile cross-room object references
Use a plain-C# MissionRunState keyed by stable identifiers
Treat loaded Unity rooms as projections rather than authoritative state owners
Apply explicit validated commands or events for durable transitions
Separate permanent checkpoint-secured temporary rollback suspend and transactional state
Prevent Unity scripts from directly mutating durable progress
Use atomic versioned MissionRunState snapshots as the primary save format
Journal checkpoint banking unique reward route objective completion and suspend transitions
Make records idempotent checksummed sequence-numbered and committed before acknowledgement
Fold journals into fresh snapshots and recover from backups
Do not journal routine combat and movement details

Performance-budget enforcement and representative stress scenes

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting the Unity CI and build-validation policy for parallel human and AI-assisted production: local developer validation only; exhaustive full-player builds and all tests on every change; or a layered gate with fast deterministic edit-mode and content validation on ordinary branches, targeted play-mode tests for affected systems, a Windows player smoke build before integration, and scheduled or milestone full regression performance save-recovery and clean-machine artifact validation. Preserve Windows-first offline delivery Unity with C# modular content packages generated registries representative stress scenes versioned saves accessibility 60 FPS target and isolated branches. Include feedback speed runner cost flaky tests Unity licensing or headless constraints artifact retention reproducibility branch protection and risk of both under-testing and overbuilding. Put the recommendation after all options. Begin a new D-221 through D-230 batch unless the cadence changes.
